text.title: Architecture Rules and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Mandatory rules for Clean Architecture + CQRS and common LLM mistakes that break layering or introduce license issues.

list.item: Architecture tests run in dotnet test — same gate as unit tests; do not relegate to a separate optional stage.
MediatR pipeline behaviors for cross-cutting concerns (validation, logging, transaction, caching) — never repeat per-handler.
required keyword + init on DTOs (.NET 7+); surfaces missing fields at compile time rather than runtime AutoMapper errors.
Migrations checked into Infrastructure project only — API project never references EF Core.
MediatR v12+ and AutoMapper v13+ are commercial products; verify SPDX license in CI before adding via dotnet add package.

antipattern.description: Agent adds behavior to Application handler instead of Domain entity method.
antipattern.reason: Business logic in handlers breaks the DDD invariant; entities become property bags and the architecture's value evaporates. Prompt agents to read Domain/Entities/ first.

antipattern.description: Agent imports Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore into the Application project.
antipattern.reason: NetArchTest catches this; without it, agents drift within weeks and the layering benefit is lost.

antipattern.description: Domain events raised at SaveChanges inside the same transaction as handlers that also write to DB.
antipattern.reason: Handler exceptions roll back the outer transaction unexpectedly. Use the outbox pattern (MassTransit) for cross-aggregate events.

antipattern.description: MediatR overused: GET /health, simple lookups wrapped in Commands/Queries.
antipattern.reason: Every MediatR dispatch pays handler resolution + pipeline behavior overhead. Reserve for use cases with cross-cutting needs (validation, auth, transactions).

antipattern.description: Entity collection mutated outside aggregate: handler does user.Posts.Add(post) directly.
antipattern.reason: Encapsulate collection mutation in aggregate methods (user.AddPost(post)); outside mutation bypasses invariants and domain events.

antipattern.description: IgnoreQueryFilters() used outside explicit admin tools.
antipattern.reason: Multi-tenant global query filter bypassed silently; data from other tenants leaks. Lint to forbid this call outside designated admin repositories.
